node_1.excerpt: The docstring of a script ( a stand - alone program ) should be usable as
node_1.fallbackText: The docstring of a script (a stand-alone program) should be usable as its “usage” message, printed when the script is invoked with incorrect or missing arguments (or perhaps with a “-h” option, for “help”). Such a docstring should document

node_10.excerpt: String literals occurring immediately after a simple assignment at the top level of a module ,
node_10.fallbackText: String literals occurring immediately after a simple assignment at the top level of a module, class, or __init__ method are called “attribute docstrings”.
node_10.nodeLabel: String literals occurring immediately after a simple assignment
node_10.textQuote: String literals occurring immediately after a simple assignment at the top level of a module, class, or __init__ method are called “attribute docstrings”.

node_11.excerpt: The aim of this PEP is to standardize the high - level structure of docstrings :
node_11.fallbackText: The aim of this PEP is to standardize the high-level structure of docstrings: what they should contain, and how to say it (without touching on any markup syntax within docstrings). The PEP contains conventions, not laws or syntax.
node_11.nodeLabel: The aim of this PEP is to standardize the high - level structure
node_11.textQuote: The aim of this PEP is to standardize the high-level structure of docstrings: what they should contain, and how to say it (without touching on any markup syntax within docstrings).

node_12.excerpt: String literals occurring immediately after another docstring are called “ additional docstrings ” .
node_12.fallbackText: String literals occurring immediately after another docstring are called “additional docstrings”.
node_12.nodeLabel: String literals occurring immediately after another docstring ar
node_12.textQuote: String literals occurring immediately after another docstring are called “additional docstrings”.

node_16.excerpt: Multi - line docstrings consist of a summary line just like a one - line docstring
node_16.fallbackText: Multi-line docstrings consist of a summary line just like a one-line docstring, followed by a blank line, followed by a more elaborate description. The summary line may be used by automatic indexing tools; it is important that it fits on on

node_3.excerpt: The docstring for a function or method should summarize its behavior and document its arguments ,
node_3.fallbackText: The docstring for a function or method should summarize its behavior and document its arguments, return value(s), side effects, exceptions raised, and restrictions on when it can be called (all if applicable). Optional arguments should be i

node_6.excerpt: “ A universal convention supplies all of maintainability , clarity , consistency , and a foundation
node_6.fallbackText: “A universal convention supplies all of maintainability, clarity, consistency, and a foundation for good programming habits too. What it doesn’t do is insist that you follow it against your will. That’s Python!”
